title: pedicularis rhinanthoides subsp. rhinanthoides
intro: Pedicularis rhinanthoides subsp. rhinanthoides, also known as Lousewort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pedicularis rhinanthoides subsp. rhinanthoides include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
